revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ichiohakayama Kofun]][[ja:市尾墓山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Ichiohakayama Kofun | native_name =市尾墓山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Ichio Hakayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ichiohakayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ichiohakayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.44277777777778|N|135.7725|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en|Takatori, Nara|qid=Q1202681|lt=Takatori, Nara|1=Takatori, Nara}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|37.85|N|135|46|28.4|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ichiohakayama Kofun'''|市尾墓山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Ichiohaka neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en|Takatori, Nara|qid=Q1202681|lt=Takatori|1=Takatori, Nara}}, [[Nara prefecture]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Together with the [[Ichio Miyazuka Kofun]], the tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217129|title=市尾墓山古墳・宮塚古墳 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ichiohakayama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at the southern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|qid=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}, between the Ochi Hills and the Ryumon Mountains. The Ichio Miyazuka Kofun is located to the southwest. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted since 1975. The tumulus is orientated to the northwest and is approximately 63 meters long, with a 45 meter wide anterior rectangular portion, and 33.5 meter diameter posterior circular portions. Both sections are approximately eight meters high. The mound was built in two tiers using sandbags. In addition to the ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones on the outside of the mound, a row of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' can be seen near the burial chamber. A projection extends from the narrow part of the mound. The mound is surrounded by a six to eight meter wide shield-shaped moat, with a 12-meter wide outer bank that is two to three meters high around the outside of the moat. The entire length of the tumulus, including the moat and outer bank, is 100 meters. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a single-sleeved horizontal stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, opening to the southwest, with a hollowed-out house-shaped Sarcophagus inside. The Sarcophagus, which is 1.2 by 2.7 meters, is one of the largest known. A large number of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been recovered from this tumulus, including beads and other jewelry, weapons, horse equipment, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery. From these grave goods and the style of the mound, this ''kofun'' was constructed in the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. As important ruins such as the Mizudoro Kofun and the remains of the Kose temple pagoda are found in the surrounding area, this tumulus has been linked to the Kose clan, a powerful late Kofun period clan who ruled this area during this period and who were active in diplomacy and military affairs with the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|qid=Q18097|lt=Korea|1=Korea}}. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, the site is open to the public after maintenance work has been completed, but access to the burial chamber is restricted. The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from {{ill|Ichio Station|en|Ichio Station|qid=Q5986275|lt=Ichio Station|1=Ichio Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|qid=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Yoshino Line|en|Yoshino Line|qid=Q901085|lt=Yoshino Line|1=Yoshino Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|qid=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:dd]]{{template-inline|Ichio Hakayama Kofun}} *[https://sightseeing2.takatori.info/hakayama/ Takatori Sightseing Guide]{{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
